sourceMovie.title: Fathers and Daughters
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Fathers and Daughters (2015) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Fathers and Daughters clicked for you, Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Girl, Interrupted
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Fathers and Daughters clicked for you, Girl, Interrupted (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: A Man Called Otto
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Fathers and Daughters clicked for you, A Man Called Otto (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Fathers and Daughters?
faq.answer: Try Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street, Girl, Interrupted, A Man Called Otto—each shares DNA with Fathers and Daughters while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Fathers and Daughters?
faq.answer: Fathers and Daughters (2015) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Fathers and Daughters worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
